transcript.rawText: What would you say to a new client? What should they expect? What are we going to do for them? Well, I think as we've mentioned, the beginning is painting the story, you know, understanding the details and what's important. They're values and they're goals. So we'll start with conversation, questions and listening. And then yes, the basic data gather where we need to know, you know, the ideas of spending and money and portfolios and all that, all the dollars and cents that go into it to then kind of marry the two where we put together the values and the goals right in line with what they have accomplished so far and help them navigate to be tax efficient and think of all the risks out there. So the process is meant to. I know I personally tend to spread it out a little. It can be daunting and it can be a lot of information. And so I always say, let's take this piece by piece. Let's start here and then we're going to focus on this and then the next thing. But I like to start by prioritizing what are your top three things. Again, what brought you to this conversation that we need to focus on right away. But then we're going to think of all those things you hadn't thought of and we're going to get to all of that. But try to spread it out a little so it's not too overwhelming.

transcript.rawText: So I often think about situations when I have clients come in and they tell me, you know, talking on a portfolio basis, I haven't looked in this at this forever. I don't know what to pick. You know, there's too many options out there. I don't even know where to start. And that on a simple level is a really great way to be able to tell them it doesn't have to be complicated, but you also don't need to be doing the research yourself. We can help with that. I think a lot of our clients, what they think of a typical financial advisor is somebody who just manages their portfolio. And what we take a look at is your whole financial life, your taxes, your state planning, retirement planning, college planning. So it's not exclusively the portfolio, that's certainly something that we take care of exclusively for clients, it's your whole financial picture. It's come in and they're very intimidated about having to create a budget. They think they're going to have to know exactly how much they spend down to every single penny. And it's just not necessary. We know how to create a financial plan for you without having you tell us exactly where you're spending every dime, every day. You're not going to have to come up with your bills that you paid, the water bill, these things are not necessary, we can take that off of your plate, absolutely. And you know again, there's so much information out there. And I think it's a great thing, it encourages people to do a little research and get the knowledge they need, but at the same time they don't know how to apply it often enough. I've had plenty of situations where they, somebody would come with a story or an idea of, oh, I read about this, yes, but that doesn't apply to you for whatever reason. And so again, it's great, but they need somebody to help them disseminate all that, you know, what really applies to me in this situation and help them find solutions that they weren't aware of. Yeah, absolutely.

transcript.rawText: We are inundated with information in today's society and I think you can get so many different opinions on if this stock is going to take off, if, you know, should I retire early should I retire later. What we do for our clients is truly gets to know what's valuable to them and to their families and what they're trying to accomplish. A lot of the noise that they hear in the media, this is not relevant to their specific situation and what they're trying to accomplish. Yeah, and I would say we are staying on top of all the changes. There's new tax laws that are coming out, there's new types of accounts that they've created. And we are staying on top of all of that and we are going to let you know if it pertains to you. And if it's beneficial to you or if it's something that you need to make a change, we're on top of it. We're going to reach out to you. You don't have to listen to any of that stuff. I think one of my favorite parts with working with my clients is when they come in and say, hey, I heard about this. I read this and I'm like, great, what did you read? And we get to talk about it. And often enough, yes, it's something that everybody's talking about now. And is this something I should be doing? Am I missing out or does this work for me? I'd love this. And that's an awesome conversation because then you can talk about the fine details, right? What it looks like for them. And yeah, actually that could be something that would work or well, unfortunately not this is why. And or the other big part to that is thinking the trade offs. You know, one of these the most common things I think these days we hear is Roth and Roth conversion. Everybody needs that, right? So for so many years, it was IRAs and 401ks. Now it's Roth, Roth, Roth. Great piece to the puzzle, but that's the point. That's a piece. It's not the solution for everybody. And so when you start to talk about what that means tax wise, what that means for savings and balances and so forth, it opens up a whole new world. And so again, I think it's it's it's very rewarding to have clients come to us with those questions because then we get to talk about all that's out there. But from a more educated point of view, and I think they love it too because they don't have to do all the research. They just say, well, you tell me what they're talking about and we can explain that so yeah.

transcript.rawText: We are inundated with information in today's society and I think you can get so many different opinions on if this stock is going to take off, if, you know, should I retire early should I retire later. What we do for our clients is truly gets to know what's valuable to them and to their families and what they're trying to accomplish. A lot of the noise that they hear in the media, this is not relevant to their specific situation and what they're trying to accomplish. Yeah, and I would say we are staying on top of all the changes. There's new tax laws that are coming out, there's new types of accounts that they've created. And we are staying on top of all of that and we are going to let you know if it pertains to you. And if it's beneficial to you or if it's something that you need to make a change, we're on top of it. We're going to reach out to you. You don't have to listen to any of that stuff. I think one of my favorite parts with working with my clients is when they come in and say, hey, I heard about this. I read this and I'm like, great, what did you read? And we get to talk about it. And often enough, yes, it's something that everybody's talking about now. And is this something I should be doing? Am I missing out or does this work for me? I'd love this. And that's an awesome conversation because then you can talk about the fine details, right? What it looks like for them. And yeah, actually that could be something that would work or well, unfortunately not this is why. And or the other big part to that is thinking the trade offs. You know, one of these the most common things I think these days we hear is Roth and Roth conversion. Everybody needs that, right? So for so many years, it was IRAs and 401ks. Now it's Roth, Roth, Roth. Great piece to the puzzle, but that's the point. That's a piece. It's not the solution for everybody. And so when you start to talk about what that means tax wise, what that means for savings and balances and so forth, it opens up a whole new world. And so again, I think it's it's it's very rewarding to have clients come to us with those questions because then we get to talk about all that's out there. But from a more educated point of view, and I think they love it too because they don't have to do all the research. They just say, well, you tell me what they're talking about and we can explain that so yeah.

So we'll start with conversation, questions and listening. And then yes, the basic data gather where we need to know, you know, the ideas of spending and money and portfolios and all that, all the dollars and cents that go into it to then kind of marry the two where we put together the values and the goals right in line with what they have accomplished so far and help them navigate to be tax efficient and think of all the risks out there. So the process is meant to. I know I personally tend to spread it out a little. It can be daunting and it can be a lot of information. And so I always say, let's take this piece by piece. Let's start here and then we're going to focus on this and then the next thing. But I like to start by prioritizing what are your top three things. Again, what brought you to this conversation that we need to focus on right away. But then we're going to think of all those things you hadn't thought of and we're going to get to all of that. But try to spread it out a little so it's not too overwhelming.

transcript.rawText: So I often think about situations when I have clients come in and they tell me, you know, talking on a portfolio basis, I haven't looked in this at this forever. I don't know what to pick. You know, there's too many options out there. I don't even know where to start. And that on a simple level is a really great way to be able to tell them it doesn't have to be complicated, but you also don't need to be doing the research yourself. We can help with that. I think a lot of our clients, what they think of a typical financial advisor is somebody who just manages their portfolio. And what we take a look at is your whole financial life, your taxes, your state planning, retirement planning, college planning. So it's not exclusively the portfolio, that's certainly something that we take care of exclusively for clients, it's your whole financial picture. It's come in and they're very intimidated about having to create a budget. They think they're going to have to know exactly how much they spend down to every single penny. And it's just not necessary. We know how to create a financial plan for you without having you tell us exactly where you're spending every dime, every day. You're not going to have to come up with your bills that you paid, the water bill, these things are not necessary, we can take that off of your plate, absolutely. And you know again, there's so much information out there. And I think it's a great thing, it encourages people to do a little research and get the knowledge they need, but at the same time they don't know how to apply it often enough. I've had plenty of situations where they, somebody would come with a story or an idea of, oh, I read about this, yes, but that doesn't apply to you for whatever reason. And so again, it's great, but they need somebody to help them disseminate all that, you know, what really applies to me in this situation and help them find solutions that they weren't aware of. Yeah, absolutely.

transcript.rawText: We are inundated with information in today's society and I think you can get so many different opinions on if this stock is going to take off, if, you know, should I retire early should I retire later. What we do for our clients is truly gets to know what's valuable to them and to their families and what they're trying to accomplish. A lot of the noise that they hear in the media, this is not relevant to their specific situation and what they're trying to accomplish. Yeah, and I would say we are staying on top of all the changes. There's new tax laws that are coming out, there's new types of accounts that they've created. And we are staying on top of all of that and we are going to let you know if it pertains to you. And if it's beneficial to you or if it's something that you need to make a change, we're on top of it. We're going to reach out to you. You don't have to listen to any of that stuff. I think one of my favorite parts with working with my clients is when they come in and say, hey, I heard about this. I read this and I'm like, great, what did you read? And we get to talk about it. And often enough, yes, it's something that everybody's talking about now. And is this something I should be doing? Am I missing out or does this work for me? I'd love this. And that's an awesome conversation because then you can talk about the fine details, right? What it looks like for them. And yeah, actually that could be something that would work or well, unfortunately not this is why. And or the other big part to that is thinking the trade offs. You know, one of these the most common things I think these days we hear is Roth and Roth conversion. Everybody needs that, right? So for so many years, it was IRAs and 401ks. Now it's Roth, Roth, Roth. Great piece to the puzzle, but that's the point. That's a piece. It's not the solution for everybody. And so when you start to talk about what that means tax wise, what that means for savings and balances and so forth, it opens up a whole new world. And so again, I think it's it's it's very rewarding to have clients come to us with those questions because then we get to talk about all that's out there. But from a more educated point of view, and I think they love it too because they don't have to do all the research. They just say, well, you tell me what they're talking about and we can explain that so yeah.
